lines.text: Tucker Traywick, East Side perimeter. We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the full moon is doing us no favors — visibility's up, which means the people with targets are seeing them just fine.
Madison-wide cumulative is holding at 61 kills as of {{CLOCK}}. That's 23 kills in this hour alone. We're tracking 6% ahead of last year's pace at this point.

East Side is pulling 38% of the city's total tonight. Atwood corridor. Schenk's Corners. The neighborhood watch Quinn mentioned at 21:00 — they're holding some perimeter near the library, but we're seeing coordinated movement south toward the residential blocks.
Bladed weapons are overrepresented in this zone. That's 14 of the 23 kills this hour — likely economic. Ammunition tariffs are real.

I'm repositioning north. The moon's too bright and I'm getting too much attention from — hold on, my rig's picking up chatter on an unsecured frequency, looks like a group organizing near the Yahara crossing and — [CUT OFF]

tickerItems.text: Madison cumulative: 61 kills. YoY delta: +6% (23 kills in hour 4 vs. 21 in 2168 hour 4).
East Side dominance continuing. Atwood + Schenk's = 38% of night's total. Bladed weapons 61% of East Side kills.
Full moon visibility impact: spotters reporting clear sightlines to 300+ meters. Target acquisition easing.
Yahara River crossing: Unconfirmed group movement south. Possible organized action. Signal lost.
